ddls:
CREATE TABLE "epidemiological_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data entry */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of data entry */,
  "area_type" TEXT /* Type of area (e.g., country, region, city) */,
  "area_code" TEXT /* Code for the area */,
  "area_name" TEXT /* Name of the area */,
  "new_cases_publish_date" INTEGER /* Number of new cases by publish date */,
  "new_cases_specimen_date" INTEGER /* Number of new cases by specimen date */,
  "disease_id" INTEGER /* ID of the disease */,
  "hospitalizations" INTEGER /* Number of new hospitalizations */,
  "deaths" INTEGER /* Number of new deaths */,
  "vaccination_status" TEXT /* Vaccination status (e.g., fully vaccinated, partially vaccinated, not vaccinated) */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the data (e.g., health department, hospital, lab) */,
  "data_quality" TEXT /* Rating or flag for data quality (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_epidemiological_data_area_name FOREIGN KEY ("area_name") REFERENCES areas ("area_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_epidemiological_data_area_code FOREIGN KEY ("area_code") REFERENCES areas ("area_code"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_epidemiological_data_disease_id FOREIGN KEY ("disease_id") REFERENCES diseases ("disease_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "areas" (
  "area_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each area */,
  "area_name" TEXT /* Name of the area */,
  "area_code" TEXT /* Code for the area */,
  "area_type" TEXT /* Type of area (e.g., country, region, city) */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Geographical latitude of the area */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Geographical longitude of the area */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Total population of the area */,
  "parent_area_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent area (for hierarchical relationships) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("area_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_areas_parent_area_id FOREIGN KEY ("parent_area_id") REFERENCES areas ("area_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "diseases" (
  "disease_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each disease */,
  "disease_name" TEXT /* Name of the disease */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the disease */,
  "transmission_mode" TEXT /* Primary mode of transmission (e.g., airborne, droplet, contact) */,
  "incubation_period" INTEGER /* Average incubation period in days */,
  "mortality_rate" REAL /* Average mortality rate */,
  "recovery_rate" REAL /* Average recovery rate */,
  "vaccine_availability" TEXT /* Availability of vaccines (e.g., yes, no, limited) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("disease_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "demographics" (
  "demographic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each demographic entry */,
  "area_id" INTEGER /* ID of the area the demographic data belongs to */,
  "age_group" TEXT /* Age group (e.g., 0-17, 18-64, 65+) */,
  "sex" TEXT /* Sex (e.g., male, female) */,
  "ethnicity" TEXT /* Ethnicity (e.g., White, Black, Asian) */,
  "population_size" INTEGER /* Population size */,
  "health_status" TEXT /* General health status (e.g., good, fair, poor) */,
  "vaccination_rate" REAL /* Vaccination rate within the demographic group */,
  "hospitalization_rate" REAL /* Hospitalization rate within the demographic group */,
  "mortality_rate" REAL /* Mortality rate within the demographic group */,
  "comorbidities" TEXT /* Common comorbidities within the demographic group */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("demographic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_demographics_area_id FOREIGN KEY ("area_id") REFERENCES areas ("area_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., administrator, researcher, public health official) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Contact phone number */,
  "address" TEXT /* Physical address */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login date and time */,
  "active_status" TEXT /* User's active status (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* JSON object or comma-separated list of permissions */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
column_names: Unique identifier for each data entry
Type of area (e.g., country, region, city)
Number of new cases by publish date
Number of new cases by specimen date
Vaccination status (e.g., fully vaccinated, partially vaccinated, not vaccinated)
Source of the data (e.g., health department, hospital, lab)
Rating or flag for data quality (e.g., high, medium, low)
Type of area (e.g., country, region, city)
ID of the parent area (for hierarchical relationships)
Primary mode of transmission (e.g., airborne, droplet, contact)
Availability of vaccines (e.g., yes, no, limited)
Unique identifier for each demographic entry
ID of the area the demographic data belongs to
General health status (e.g., good, fair, poor)
Vaccination rate within the demographic group
Hospitalization rate within the demographic group
Mortality rate within the demographic group
Common comorbidities within the demographic group
Role of the user (e.g., administrator, researcher, public health official)
JSON object or comma-separated list of permissions
